"Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison." (Matt. 14:3.) John the Baptist had been preaching in Peraea in the tetrarchy of Herod Antipas, and he had denounced Antipas's adulterous and incestuous relationship with Herodias, his half-brother Philip's former wife. Herodias was responsible for John's arrest. The New Testament itself cites no specific place for the…
